page: Microsoft Azure L16s_v3 is a storage and memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the LSv3 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. This configuration delivers 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor features a cache hierarchy including 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 caches, alongside hardware support for AVX-512 vector instructions and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA extensions.

The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It features 4004 GB of bundled local storage, making it highly capable for storage-intensive workloads. In benchmark testing, the memory subsystem demonstrated strong performance, placing in the top 25% of the Passmark memory score with a read-only cached small-block bandwidth of 834.55 GB/sec. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth reaches 100.34 GB/sec for reads and 58.45 GB/sec for writes.

Compute and application benchmarks position the L16s_v3 in the average tier. It achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 10151.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 21313.63. For database and web operations, the server processed 15.98 million Redis SET operations per second and sustained a static web serving throughput of 21.98 GB/sec. Small-scale LLM inference using a 7B model yielded 35.13 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 12.38 tokens/sec for text generation.

Given its high local storage capacity and strong memory performance, the L16s_v3 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring dense local data caching without the overhead of external storage attachments. This virtual machine is suited for relational databases, NoSQL data stores, big data analytics, and transactional processing systems.
description: Microsoft Azure L16s_v3 is a storage-optimized virtual machine from the LSv3 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM and 4004 GB of bundled local storage, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system delivers strong memory performance, scoring in the top 25% on Passmark memory benchmarks, with a cached read bandwidth of 834.55 GB/sec. Compute and database performance remain in the average tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 21313.63 and a Redis throughput of 15.98 million SET operations per second. L16s_v3 is designed for storage-intensive and database workloads that benefit from high local storage capacity and balanced memory-to-CPU ratios.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L16s_v3 is a storage-optimized server featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 4004 GB of local storage, delivering strong memory performance for database and transactional workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L16s_v3 is a storage-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 4004 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-dense virtual machine featuring high-capacity local storage and strong memory performance for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 48 MB L3 cache
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
4004 GB of bundled local storage for high-capacity data caching
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of Passmark benchmarks
Suited for relational databases, NoSQL data stores, and transactional workloads
